methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $66M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
Elementary Robotics, a Series C robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $66M to date, is evaluated against a peer set of nine same-sector and adjacent-stage private companies, supplemented by one public comparable. The private peer universe includes later-stage funded robotics firms such as 1X Technologies ($10.0B Series B), Exotec ($2.0B Series D), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B Series B), Dexterity ($1.6B Series B), and Hadrian ($1.6B Series C), with peer valuations ranging from $300M to $10.0B.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised ($66M) as a proxy baseline and applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C to next-stage advancement, implies a valuation of $112M. Peer-median analysis across the nine-company robotics cohort yields a median valuation of $1.6B, with the same $300M-to-$10.0B range reflecting significant dispersion within the sector and stage band.

The triangulated analysis produces a range of $95M to $1.8B, with a median of $856M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty regarding Elementary Robotics' positioning relative to better-capitalized peers and the heterogeneous outcomes in early-stage robotics funding. The stage-progression method anchors conservatively; peer-median dominates the upper bound. Key uncertainties include company-specific revenue traction, IP differentiation, and realized capital efficiency relative to the peer cohort.
